title: galactia incana
intro: Galactia incana, also known as the wild milk pea or wild sensitive plant, is a perennial vine native to North America. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate the secretion of milk in lactating women and to treat conditions such as dysmenorrhea and menstrual cramps. Galactia incana has been used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, skin irritations, and as an antispasmodic agent to relieve muscle spasms. The herbal preparation of Galactia incana typically involves drying and crushing the leaves, which can then be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
